Brown v. Board of Education

Opinion by Chief Justice Warren

347 U.S. 483 | Download PDF | Cited by 1000

MR. CHIEF JUSTICE WARREN delivered the opinion of the Court.

This synthetic record combines several educational access questions for one fictional review.

The represented students request a neutral school placement process under the invented record.

The parties dispute whether the described policy satisfies the imaginary equal-treatment requirement.

We conclude that in the field of public education the doctrine of separate but equal has no place. Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal.

This synthetic judicial example should be read with its fictional record, arguments, and governing rule.

The judgment of the court below is reversed for this synthetic record and returned for review.
